gene_description: AKT Serine/Threonine Kinase 1 — key signaling molecule in the dopamine D2 receptor pathway downstream of cannabinoid receptor activation. Modulates cannabis-induced dopamine release and psychosis risk.

source.title: Confirmation that the AKT1 (rs2494732) genotype influences the risk of psychosis in cannabis users
source.finding: C/C carriers of rs2494732 with cannabis use had 2.18-fold increased risk of psychotic disorder vs T/T carriers. Among daily cannabis users, C/C genotype showed 7.23-fold increase in psychosis odds.

CC.effect: Homozygous for the C allele. In cannabis users, this genotype confers a 2.18-fold increased risk of psychotic disorder compared to T/T carriers. Among daily cannabis users, the risk escalates dramatically to 7.23-fold. The mechanism involves excess dopamine release via the AKT1-GSK3 signaling cascade upon cannabinoid receptor activation. Importantly, the variant alone does not increase psychosis risk — the effect emerges through gene-environment interaction with cannabis exposure.
CC.recommendation: Strongly counsel against recreational cannabis use. If medical cannabinoids are necessary, use CBD-only preparations when possible and avoid THC. If THC is required, use minimum effective doses with close psychiatric monitoring. Screen for psychosis risk factors before initiating any cannabinoid therapy.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate risk of cannabis-induced psychotic symptoms between CC and TT genotypes. The interaction between AKT1 genotype and cannabis frequency determines risk level — occasional use carries lower risk than daily use.
CT.recommendation: Advise caution with cannabis use, especially frequent or daily use. If medical cannabinoids are prescribed, prefer lower THC content and monitor for psychiatric symptoms. Consider CBD-dominant formulations.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate risk of cannabis-induced psychotic symptoms between CC and TT genotypes. The interaction between AKT1 genotype and cannabis frequency determines risk level — occasional use carries lower risk than daily use.
TC.recommendation: Advise caution with cannabis use, especially frequent or daily use. If medical cannabinoids are prescribed, prefer lower THC content and monitor for psychiatric symptoms. Consider CBD-dominant formulations.

TT.effect: Homozygous for the T allele. Lowest genetic risk for cannabis-induced psychosis among AKT1 genotypes. Normal AKT1-GSK3 signaling in response to cannabinoid receptor activation. Standard dopaminergic response to cannabis exposure.
TT.recommendation: Standard risk assessment for cannabinoid therapy. General precautions still apply regarding cannabis use and mental health monitoring.
